# SCORE Advanced Programs for Small Businesses

Last reviewed for BOSS Library ingestion: 2026-06-21

## BOSS Library tags
score, advanced-programs, growth, ceo-forum, advisory-board

## Resource purpose
SCORE advanced programs such as CEO forums, advisory boards, pitch competitions, awards, women-focused programs, and conferences.

## How the BOSS AI Librarian should use this resource
Use for more mature entrepreneurs who need peer learning, advisory support, growth visibility, pitch readiness, or executive-level accountability.

## Best-fit BOSS lifecycle stage
Mature Operations, Growth, and sometimes Transition/Exit. Not usually first-route for raw Ideation.

## Best audience
Existing small business owners, growth-stage entrepreneurs, CEOs, and founders ready for advanced support.

## Route the user here when
The business already has operations, revenue, leadership challenges, growth questions, pitch preparation needs, or a need for peer advisory support.

## Do not over-route here when
Do not route a raw idea-stage entrepreneur here before concept clarification, validation, and basic launch planning unless they specifically ask about advanced SCORE programs.

## BOSS routing notes
- Eligibility, availability, local chapter participation, program calendars, and application details can change; route to live page for current requirements.
- For Houston clients, check whether SCORE Houston or nearby chapters offer relevant advanced programming.

## Related SCORE links
- SCORE Houston: https://www.score.org/tx/houston/
- SCORE mentoring: https://www.score.org/mentors/
- Manage + Grow hub: https://www.score.org/manage-grow/

## Response pattern for BOSS
Use this resource as a routing destination, not as a substitute for professional legal, tax, lending, procurement, or compliance advice. For time-sensitive workshops, deadlines, prices, contact details, or eligibility rules, send the user to the live source URL first.
